Welcome to ACSAC 42!Submissions are open

Submit a technical paper, case study, panel, poster, wip, or workshop. Scroll down for all deadlines.

Call for Submissions

Join Us in
Los Angeles
for ACSAC 42!

The IEEE Annual Computer Security Applications Conference (ACSAC) brings together leading researchers and practitioners, along with a diverse group of security professionals drawn from academia, industry, and government, gathered to present and discuss the latest cybersecurity results and topics. With peer reviewed technical papers, invited talks, panels, national interest discussions, and workshops, ACSAC continues its core mission of investigating practical solutions for computer and network security technologies.

As an internationally recognized forum where researchers, practitioners, and developers meet to learn and to exchange practical ideas and experiences in computer and network security, we invite you to submit your work. In addition to peer-reviewed papers on novel applied research, we also welcome case studies on real-world applications, panels featuring world experts, and workshops consisting of 1-2 day sessions on hot cybersecurity and privacy topics.

Submission Deadlines

Technical Papers

May 26

Case Studies

September 22

Panels

July 8

Posters and WiPs

September 19

Workshop Proposals

May 26

Artifacts Competition

October 17

Student Conferenceships

September 26

Hard Topic Theme: Security and Privacy of Agentic Systems

For this year's hard topic, we invite work that advances our understanding of the security and privacy of agentic systems. The rapid adoption of AI-driven agents—systems that autonomously invoke tools and interact with their environment—introduces a new attack surface that demands attention from the security community. Threats range from prompt injection and goal manipulation to as-yet-unknown vulnerabilities and data leakage arising from the interactive and autonomous nature of these systems.

Read More
